name: National Debt Group
description_short: National Debt Group is a debt settlement company offering negotiation services for consumers with $10,000+ in unsecured debt. Limited online presence and verifiable credentials. Not to be confused with National Debt Relief.
description_long:
National Debt Group is a debt settlement company that offers creditor negotiation services for consumers carrying significant unsecured debt. The company should not be confused with National Debt Relief, which is a much larger and more established debt settlement provider with a BBB A+ rating and over 5,500 BBB reviews. National Debt Group operates as a separate, smaller entity in the debt settlement space with a substantially less documented track record.

The company's services follow the standard debt settlement model: consumers enroll their unsecured debts (credit cards, medical bills, personal loans), stop making payments to creditors, accumulate funds in a dedicated savings account, and have the company's negotiators pursue reduced lump-sum settlements. Under FTC regulation, settlement fees can only be charged after a successful settlement is reached. Typical industry fees range from 15-25% of enrolled debt, though National Debt Group's specific fee structure is not prominently disclosed.

A primary concern with National Debt Group is the limited verifiable information available through standard consumer protection channels. The company's online presence is minimal compared to established competitors, and clear BBB profile information, CFPB complaint data, and independent review aggregation are difficult to locate under this specific company name. This lack of transparency makes due diligence challenging for consumers and is itself a risk factor when choosing a company to manage sensitive financial negotiations on your behalf.

Consumers considering debt settlement have multiple well-documented alternatives. National Debt Relief (BBB A+, 4.74/5 from 5,500+ BBB reviews) is the industry leader with extensive third-party verification. ClearOne Advantage (BBB A+, accredited since 2024), TurboDebt (BBB A+, 4.9 Google stars from 7,800+ reviews), and Achieve (formerly Freedom Debt Relief, $18B+ settled) all offer comparable settlement services with substantially stronger public accountability records. Before committing to settlement, consumers should evaluate whether debt consolidation loans from credit unions or online lenders could achieve a similar outcome without credit damage. Credit counseling through nonprofit NFCC-member agencies provides free budgeting guidance and debt management plans at reduced interest rates. Credit monitoring services help track credit health during any debt resolution process. A debt payoff calculator can help consumers model whether settlement, consolidation, or accelerated direct repayment best fits their specific situation.

pros: Debt settlement model charges fees only after successful negotiation per FTC regulation
May offer free initial consultation and debt evaluation
Settlement can potentially reduce total debt owed by 30-50% in successful cases
cons: Extremely limited verifiable information available through consumer protection channels
No prominent BBB profile, making complaint resolution history difficult to verify
No CFPB complaint data found, limiting transparency about consumer experience patterns
Easily confused with National Debt Relief, a much larger and better-documented company
No publicly available pricing, success rates, or program timeline information
Debt settlement inherently requires stopping creditor payments, causing serious credit score damage
best_for: Consumers are strongly advised to compare National Debt Relief (BBB A+, 4.74/5 stars), ClearOne Advantage, or TurboDebt before considering this provider
Only appropriate for consumers who have personally verified the company's credentials, licensing, and fee structure
diagnosis: National Debt Group lacks the verifiable credentials needed for a confident assessment. With minimal online presence, no prominent BBB profile, and no CFPB data under this name, consumers face significant information asymmetry. The debt settlement market has well-documented alternatives: National Debt Relief (A+, 5,500+ BBB reviews), ClearOne Advantage (A+), and TurboDebt (A+, 7,800+ Google reviews) all provide substantially better transparency. Not recommended without thorough personal verification of licensing, fees, and contractual terms.
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
